definition: The Sustainability Initiative of South Africa, a non-profit organization established in 2008 by the South African fruit industry to streamline ethical trade and environmental compliance for agricultural suppliers.
Secure Hash Algorithm 256-bit, a cryptographic hash function that generates a unique, fixed-size 256-bit hash value from any input data, used for data integrity checks.
A digital twin for a physical product that provides comprehensive information about the product's lifecycle, accessible by various stakeholders to retrieve information and verify credentials.

In the context of Digital Product Passports, a verifiable credential (like a hashed SIZA certificate) that provides an immutable and verifiable link to the ethical and environmental credentials of a product, ensuring data integrity and authenticity.

term: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD)
definition: A legislative effort by the European Union to mandate responsible corporate behavior across global value chains, requiring companies to identify, prevent, mitigate, and account for adverse human rights and environmental impacts.
The Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013, a South African law that sets conditions for the lawful processing of personal information, ensuring privacy and data protection.
GLOBALG.A.P. Risk Assessment on Social Practice, a voluntary add-on module to GLOBALG.A.P. certification designed to assess social practices on farms, including worker health & safety and welfare.
Social Accountability 8000, a comprehensive, auditable international standard for social accountability that provides a framework for fair treatment of workers across all industries.
